info.name: Gmail Buddy API
info.description: Complete API collection for testing Gmail Buddy endpoints with OAuth2 authentication

request.description: Retrieve all Gmail messages for the authenticated user
Retrieve the latest 50 Gmail messages
Filter messages based on criteria like sender, subject, etc.
Get the body content of a specific message. Set messageId variable first.
Mark a specific message as read. Set messageId variable first.
Delete a single message by ID. Set messageId variable first.
Delete multiple messages based on filter criteria
Add or remove labels from messages matching filter criteria

item.name: Send & Draft

body.raw:
{ "to": ["hiring-manager@bigcorp.example"], "cc": ["referrer@example.com"], "bcc": [], "subject": "Application for Senior Backend Engineer — referred by Sam", "body": "

Dear hiring team,

Sam suggested I reach out...

", "bodyType": "html", "threadId": null, "inReplyToMessageId": null, "attachments": [] }

request.description:
Stages an email as a draft in the user's Gmail Drafts folder. Returns 201 Created with a Location header and a DraftResponse body. The draft is immediately visible in Gmail for review, editing, or discard. This is the recommended path for AI-personalized outreach content. Requires gmail.send scope (or mail.google.com).

New optional fields (feature 002):
- threadId: Gmail short thread ID (hex). Places the draft in an existing thread. Omit or set null for new conversations.
- inReplyToMessageId: Gmail short message ID of the message being replied to. Triggers a users.messages.get metadata lookup that populates In-Reply-To and References MIME headers. Returns 422 if the message doesn't exist.
- attachments: Array of base64-encoded file attachments. Each element requires filename, mimeType, and base64Data. Total payload (body + attachments) capped at 25 MB.

response.name: 201 Created — Draft staged successfully

body.raw:
{ "to": ["hiring-manager@bigcorp.example"], "cc": ["referrer@example.com"], "bcc": [], "subject": "Application for Senior Backend Engineer — referred by Sam", "body": "

Dear hiring team,

Sam suggested I reach out...

", "bodyType": "html" }

response.name: 400 Bad Request — Validation failure
422 Unprocessable Entity — Original message not found
response.body:
{
    "type": "/problems/original-message-not-found",
    "title": "Original Message Not Found",
    "status": 422,
    "detail": "The message referenced by inReplyToMessageId '0000000000000000' does not exist or is not accessible in the authenticated account",
    "instance": "/api/v1/gmail/drafts",
    "retryable": false,
    "category": "CLIENT_ERROR"
}

request.description: Delivers a previously-created draft by its identifier. This is a state transition on an existing resource — the draft is consumed and the message appears in the Sent folder. Returns 200 OK (not 201) with no Location header. Naturally idempotent: a retry after successful send returns 404. Requires gmail.send scope (or mail.google.com). Set the draftId collection variable to the value returned by POST /drafts.

response.name: 200 OK — Draft sent successfully
404 Not Found — Draft already sent or discarded
response.body:
{
    "type": "/problems/resource-not-found",
    "title": "Resource Not Found",
    "status": 404,
    "detail": "Draft not found — already sent, discarded, or invalid draftId",
    "instance": "/api/v1/gmail/drafts/r-9876543210/send"
}

{

    "cc": [],
    "bcc": [],
    "subject": "Following up on our conversation",
    "body": "Hi,\n\nThanks for taking the time to chat yesterday...\n\nBest,\nOmar",

    "attachments": []
}

request.description:
Sends an email immediately via the Gmail API. Reserved for deterministic, pre-trusted templates — not the recommended path for AI-personalized content (use POST /drafts instead). Returns 201 Created with a Location header pointing to the message body endpoint. WARNING: callers must not auto-retry after a timeout — duplicate sends may result. Requires gmail.send scope (or mail.google.com).

New optional fields (feature 002):
- threadId: Gmail short thread ID (hex). Places the message in an existing thread. Omit or set null for new conversations.
- inReplyToMessageId: Gmail short message ID of the message being replied to. Triggers a users.messages.get metadata lookup that populates In-Reply-To and References MIME headers. Returns 422 if the message doesn't exist. Quota: ~105 units when supplied (vs 100 for non-threaded).
- attachments: Array of base64-encoded file attachments. Each element requires filename, mimeType, and base64Data. Total payload (body + attachments) capped at 25 MB.

response.name: 201 Created — Message sent successfully

body.raw:
{
    "to": ["recruiter@example.com"],
    "cc": [],
    "bcc": [],
    "subject": "Following up on our conversation",
    "body": "Hi,\n\nThanks for taking the time to chat yesterday...\n\nBest,\nOmar",
    "bodyType": "text"
}

response.name: 400 Bad Request — Validation failure
422 Unprocessable Entity — Original message not found
response.body:
{
    "type": "/problems/original-message-not-found",
    "title": "Original Message Not Found",
    "status": 422,
    "detail": "The message referenced by inReplyToMessageId '0000000000000000' does not exist or is not accessible in the authenticated account",
    "instance": "/api/v1/gmail/messages",
    "retryable": false,
    "category": "CLIENT_ERROR"
}

request.description:
Recipe 1 from quickstart.md — send a threaded reply that appears in the same Gmail conversation as the original message.

Before running:
1. Set originalMsgId to a real Gmail short message ID from your mailbox (obtain via GET /api/v1/gmail/messages/latest).
2. Set originalThreadId to the corresponding thread ID.
3. Set testRecipient to an inbox you control.

Expected: HTTP 201, X-Gmail-Quota-Used: 105 (5 lookup + 100 send).
Verify: In Gmail, the reply is nested under the original thread. Gmail 'Show original' shows In-Reply-To and References headers.

response.name: 201 Created — Threaded reply sent (X-Gmail-Quota-Used: 105)

request.description:
Recipe 3 from quickstart.md — send a message with a PDF attachment (no threading).

Before running:
1. Set testRecipient to an inbox you control.
2. Set pdfBase64 to a valid standard Base64 string (not URL-safe). For a minimal test use JVBERi0xLjQK (a 9-byte PDF stub). For a real file: base64 -i resume.pdf | tr -d '\n'.

Expected: HTTP 201, X-Gmail-Quota-Used: 100 (attachments do not add quota cost).
Verify: Recipient's inbox shows a paperclip icon; the attachment is downloadable and byte-for-byte identical to the source file.

response.name: 201 Created — Message with attachment sent (X-Gmail-Quota-Used: 100)

request.description:
Recipe 4 from quickstart.md — threaded reply WITH an attachment (US3 combined scenario).

Before running:
1. Set originalMsgId to a real Gmail short message ID from your mailbox (obtain via GET /api/v1/gmail/messages/latest).
2. Set originalThreadId to the corresponding thread ID.
3. Set testRecipient to an inbox you control.
4. Set pdfBase64 to a valid standard Base64 string. Minimal test value: JVBERi0xLjQK

Expected: HTTP 201, X-Gmail-Quota-Used: 105 (threading lookup costs 5 units; attachments do not add extra quota).
Verify: The reply appears nested in the original thread (threading) AND has a paperclip icon (attachment). Gmail 'Show original' confirms both In-Reply-To/References headers and multipart/mixed content structure.

response.name: 201 Created — Threaded reply with attachment sent (X-Gmail-Quota-Used: 105)

query.description: Items per page. Min 1, max 50. Default 25.
Opaque pagination token from a prior response's nextPageToken. Omit for the first page.

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of pending drafts for the authenticated user. Each item in `results` is a DraftListItem summary (id, recipients, subject, snippet, threadId, attachmentCount). Use `nextPageToken` to fetch subsequent pages. An empty Drafts folder returns HTTP 200 with an empty `results` array — not 404.

Quota cost: 1 unit (list call) + N×5 units (per-item get calls). For a full page of 25 items: 126 units.

Feature 003 (drafts-crud). Requires gmail.modify scope (already in the project scope set).

response.name: 200 OK — Drafts listed successfully

400 Bad Request — Invalid limit parameter

variable.description: Gmail draft ID. Must match [0-9a-fA-F]{1,32}. Obtain from the id field in List Drafts response.

request.description:
Retrieves the full content of a single draft including body text, all recipients, threading metadata (threadId, inReplyToMessageId), and attachment metadata (filename, mimeType, sizeBytes — binary data is NOT returned). Use this endpoint after List Drafts to inspect a specific draft before sending, updating, or deleting it.

Quota cost: 5 units per call (users.drafts.get with format=FULL).

Feature 003 (drafts-crud). Requires gmail.modify scope.

response.name: 200 OK — Full draft detail with attachment

Hi,

I wanted to follow up on my application for the Senior Backend Engineer role.

", "bodyType": "html", "threadId": "1976a4bc3fe89d0c", "inReplyToMessageId": "1976a4bc3fe89d0c", "attachments": [ { "filename": "resume-2026.pdf", "mimeType": "application/pdf", "sizeBytes": 245760 } ] }

variable.description: Gmail draft ID. Must match [0-9a-fA-F]{1,32}. Obtain from the id field in List Drafts response.

request.description:
Permanently removes a draft from the authenticated user's Gmail Drafts folder. There is no trash or soft-delete — the draft is gone immediately. Returns 204 No Content with an empty response body on success.

Idempotency note: a 404 response indicates the draft is already absent (sent, previously deleted, or never existed). Cleanup workflows may treat 404 as success-equivalent per the contract spec.

Quota cost: 10 units per call (users.drafts.delete).

Feature 003 (drafts-crud). Requires gmail.modify scope.

response.name: 204 No Content — Draft deleted successfully
404 Not Found — Draft already deleted, sent, or never existed
response.body:
{
    "type": "/problems/resource-not-found",
    "title": "Resource Not Found",
    "status": 404,
    "detail": "Draft '0000000000000000' was not found or has already been sent",
    "instance": "/api/v1/gmail/drafts/0000000000000000",
    "retryable": false,
    "category": "CLIENT_ERROR"
}

response.name: 400 Bad Request — Invalid draft ID format

body.raw:
{ "to": ["hiring-manager@bigcorp.example"], "cc": [], "bcc": [], "subject": "Following up \u2014 Senior Backend Engineer application (revised)", "body": "

Hi,

I wanted to follow up and share a revised cover letter.

", "bodyType": "html", "threadId": null, "inReplyToMessageId": null, "attachments": [] }

variable.description: Gmail draft ID. Must match [0-9a-fA-F]{1,32}. Obtain from the id field in List Drafts response.

request.description:
Replaces the entire content of an existing draft with a new SendMessageDTO payload. PUT semantics: the new content fully replaces the old content — no partial update. If attachments are omitted from the request body, the updated draft will have no attachments even if the original had some.

The request body accepts the same SendMessageDTO shape as POST /api/v1/gmail/drafts, including all optional fields: cc, bcc, threadId, inReplyToMessageId, and attachments (base64-encoded). All the same validation rules apply (email format, size limits, header injection checks, 25 MB payload cap).

Returns 200 OK with a DraftDetailResponse showing the post-update state — the same shape as GET /api/v1/gmail/drafts/{id}. No follow-up GET call is needed.

Quota cost: 15 units (users.drafts.update). Add 5 extra units if inReplyToMessageId is supplied (threading metadata lookup).

Feature 003 (drafts-crud). Requires gmail.modify scope.

response.name: 200 OK — Draft updated (subject + body, no attachments)
response.body:
{ "id": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7890", "to": ["hiring-manager@bigcorp.example"], "cc": [], "bcc": [], "subject": "Following up \u2014 Senior Backend Engineer application (revised)", "body": "

Hi,

I wanted to follow up and share a revised cover letter.

", "bodyType": "html", "threadId": null, "inReplyToMessageId": null, "attachments": [] }

response.name: 200 OK — Draft updated with attachment
response.body:
{ "id": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7890", "to": ["hiring-manager@bigcorp.example"], "cc": [], "bcc": [], "subject": "Application \u2014 Senior Backend Engineer (r\u00e9sum\u00e9 attached)", "body": "

Hi,

Please find my r\u00e9sum\u00e9 attached.

", "bodyType": "html", "threadId": null, "inReplyToMessageId": null, "attachments": [ { "filename": "resume-2026-v2.pdf", "mimeType": "application/pdf", "sizeBytes": 249856 } ] }

item.description:
Send emails directly or stage them as drafts for review. All three endpoints require gmail.send scope (or the broader mail.google.com scope). POST /drafts is the recommended default path for AI-personalized outreach.

Feature 002 (threading-attachments) adds three optional fields to POST /messages and POST /drafts:
- threadId: places the outgoing message in an existing thread
- inReplyToMessageId: triggers RFC 5322 In-Reply-To / References header construction (costs ~5 extra quota units)
- attachments: base64-encoded file attachments; total payload capped at 25 MB

Feature 003 (drafts-crud) adds four endpoints for managing existing drafts: GET /drafts (list), GET /drafts/{id} (detail), DELETE /drafts/{id} (cancel), PUT /drafts/{id} (replace content). All four require gmail.modify scope.

item.name: Read API

query.description: Items per page. Min 1, max 100. Default 50.
Opaque pagination token from a prior response's nextPageToken. Omit for the first page.
Filter by sender email address (optional, max 255 chars).
Filter by recipient email address (optional, max 255 chars).
Filter by subject fragment (optional, max 255 chars).
Raw Gmail search syntax (optional, max 500 chars).
Exclusion query in Gmail search syntax (optional, max 500 chars).
Filter for threads that include attachments (optional boolean).

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of conversation threads for the authenticated user. All filter parameters are optional and can be combined. The response always returns HTTP 200 — empty results (no matching threads) return `{results: [], nextPageToken: null, totalCount: 0}`, never 404.

Quota cost: 10 units flat (one `users.threads.list` call regardless of page size — no N+1).

Validation rules:
- `limit`: 1–100 (default 50)
- `pageToken`: max 255 characters
- `from` / `to`: valid email format, max 255 characters
- `subject`: max 255 characters
- `query` / `negatedQuery`: max 500 characters, valid Gmail search syntax

Feature 004 (read-api-completeness). Requires gmail.readonly scope (already covered by gmail.modify).

response.name: 200 OK — Threads listed with results
200 OK — Empty results (no matching threads)
400 Bad Request — Invalid limit parameter
response.body:
{
    "type": "/problems/validation-error",
    "title": "Validation Error",
    "status": 400,
    "detail": "Request validation failed",
    "instance": "/api/v1/gmail/threads",
    "retryable": false,
    "category": "CLIENT_ERROR"
}

variable.description: Gmail thread ID. Must match [0-9a-fA-F]{1,32}. Obtain from the id field in List Threads response.

request.description:
Retrieves all messages in a conversation thread, including headers, body, label IDs, and attachment metadata for each message. Messages are returned in chronological ascending order (oldest first — matches Gmail UI's conversation view).

Response shape notes:
- `labelIds` at the thread level is the UNION of all per-message label sets (deduplicated).
- Each message's `headers` contains only the 9 whitelisted RFC 5322 canonical headers that are present in the source message (missing headers are absent from the map, not null).
- Quota cost: 10 units per call (`users.threads.get` with `format=FULL`).

Path parameter validation: `threadId` must match `[0-9a-fA-F]{1,32}` (same hex format as message IDs). A non-hex or oversized ID returns 400.

Feature 004 (read-api-completeness). Requires gmail.readonly scope.

response.name: 200 OK — Full thread with nested messages
response.body:
{ "threadId": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7890", "labelIds": ["INBOX", "CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "Label_42"], "messages": [ { "id": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7891", "threadId": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7890", "headers": { "From": "recruiter@bigcorp.example", "To": "you@example.com", "Subject": "Backend Engineer opportunity at BigCorp", "Date": "Mon, 05 May 2026 10:00:00 -0700", "Message-ID": "" }, "snippet": "Hi, I wanted to reach out about an exciting opportunity...", "body": "

Hi,

I wanted to reach out about a Backend Engineer opportunity at BigCorp...

", "bodyType": "html", "labelIds": ["INBOX", "CATEGORY_PERSONAL"], "attachments": [] }, { "id": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7892", "threadId": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7890", "headers": { "From": "recruiter@bigcorp.example", "To": "you@example.com", "Subject": "Re: Backend Engineer opportunity at BigCorp", "Date": "Wed, 07 May 2026 14:23:00 -0700", "In-Reply-To": "", "References": "", "Message-ID": "" }, "snippet": "Following up \u2014 please find the job description attached.", "body": "

Following up \u2014 please find the job description attached.

", "bodyType": "html", "labelIds": ["INBOX", "CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "Label_42"], "attachments": [ { "attachmentId": "ANGjdJ8BwFpn3nQ0oFQ7wPjVLfRxAbCdEfGhIjKlMnOpQrStUvWxYz", "filename": "BigCorp-JD-Backend-Engineer.pdf", "mimeType": "application/pdf", "sizeBytes": 183296 } ] } ] }

query.description: Response format: 'full' (default, 10 quota units — body + headers + attachments) or 'metadata' (5 quota units — headers + labels only, body=null). Case-insensitive. Values other than 'full' or 'metadata' return 400.

variable.description: Gmail message ID. Must match [0-9a-fA-F]{1,32}. Obtain from List Threads or Get Thread Detail responses.

request.description:
Retrieves full structured detail for a single message including headers, body, label IDs, and attachment metadata. The optional `?format=` query parameter controls the level of detail and the Gmail API quota cost:

- `format=full` (default): returns body text, all 9 whitelisted RFC 5322 headers, and attachment metadata. Costs 10 quota units.
- `format=metadata`: returns only the 9 whitelisted headers and label IDs. `body` and `bodyType` are null; `attachments` is empty. Costs 5 quota units — use for quick label/header checks.

Note: This endpoint is distinct from the legacy `GET /messages/{id}/body` endpoint (which returns raw body text only). This endpoint returns the full structured `MessageDetailResponse` shape.

Path parameter validation: `messageId` must match `[0-9a-fA-F]{1,32}`.
Query parameter validation: `format` must be `full` or `metadata` (case-insensitive). `?format=raw` returns 400.

Feature 004 (read-api-completeness). Requires gmail.readonly scope.

response.name: 200 OK — Full message detail (format=full, default)
response.body:
{ "id": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7892", "threadId": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7890", "headers": { "From": "recruiter@bigcorp.example", "To": "you@example.com", "Subject": "Re: Backend Engineer opportunity at BigCorp", "Date": "Wed, 07 May 2026 14:23:00 -0700", "In-Reply-To": "", "References": "", "Message-ID": "" }, "snippet": "Following up \u2014 please find the job description attached.", "body": "

Following up \u2014 please find the job description attached.

", "bodyType": "html", "labelIds": ["INBOX", "CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "Label_42"], "attachments": [ { "attachmentId": "ANGjdJ8BwFpn3nQ0oFQ7wPjVLfRxAbCdEfGhIjKlMnOpQrStUvWxYz", "filename": "BigCorp-JD-Backend-Engineer.pdf", "mimeType": "application/pdf", "sizeBytes": 183296 } ] }

response.name: 200 OK — Metadata only (format=metadata, cheaper quota)
response.body:
{
    "id": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7892",
    "threadId": "1a2b3c4d5e6f7890",
    "headers": {
        "From": "recruiter@bigcorp.example",
        "To": "you@example.com",
        "Subject": "Re: Backend Engineer opportunity at BigCorp",
        "Date": "Wed, 07 May 2026 14:23:00 -0700",
        "Message-ID": "<CABc456abc@mail.gmail.com>"
    },
    "snippet": "Following up \u2014 please find the job description attached.",
    "body": null,
    "bodyType": null,
    "labelIds": ["INBOX", "CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "Label_42"],
    "attachments": []
}

request.description:
Enumerates all visible labels for the authenticated user, including system labels (INBOX, SENT, TRASH, SPAM, CATEGORY_PERSONAL, etc.) and user-created labels.

No query parameters accepted — Gmail's `users.labels.list` does not paginate. All labels are returned in a single response. The response has `totalCount` but NO `nextPageToken` field.

Quota cost: 1 unit per call (`users.labels.list`).

Use label IDs from this response as input to GET /labels/{id} for full detail (counts, color).

Feature 004 (read-api-completeness). Requires gmail.readonly scope.

response.name: 200 OK — Labels listed (system + user labels)
401 Unauthorized — Bearer token invalid or expired
response.body:
{
    "type": "/problems/authentication-failed",
    "title": "Authentication Failed",
    "status": 401,
    "detail": "Bearer token is invalid or expired",
    "instance": "/api/v1/gmail/labels",
    "retryable": false,
    "category": "CLIENT_ERROR"
}

variable.description: Gmail label ID. Must match [A-Za-z0-9_]{1,128}. Examples: 'INBOX', 'SENT', 'Label_42'. Obtain from List Labels response. Labels with hyphens or slashes are rejected with 400.

request.description:
Retrieves full metadata for a specific label including message/thread counts and color (for user-created labels that have a color assigned).

Path parameter validation: `labelId` must match `[A-Za-z0-9_]{1,128}` (covers system labels like INBOX/SENT and user labels like Label_42). Labels with hyphens, slashes, or other special characters return 400.

Response notes:
- `color` field is absent from the JSON (not null) when no color is set on the label (`@JsonInclude(NON_NULL)` globally configured). Use field-existence checks, not null checks.
- System labels (INBOX, SENT, etc.) never have colors.
- `messagesTotal`, `messagesUnread`, `threadsTotal`, `threadsUnread` are populated from Gmail API counts.

Quota cost: 1 unit per call (`users.labels.get`).

Feature 004 (read-api-completeness). Requires gmail.readonly scope.

variable.description: Gmail message ID. Must match [0-9a-fA-F]{1,32}. Obtain from List Threads or Get Thread Detail responses.

request.description:
Retrieves the metadata for all attachments on a specific message — without downloading binary content. Returns an array of `MessageAttachmentMetadata` items with `attachmentId`, `filename`, `mimeType`, and `sizeBytes`.

Important: returns HTTP 200 with `{results: [], totalCount: 0}` when the message has no attachments — NOT 404. Use this to check whether a message has attachments before calling the download endpoint.

Use the `attachmentId` values from this response as the path parameter for the Download Attachment endpoint.

Path parameter validation: `messageId` must match `[0-9a-fA-F]{1,32}`.

Quota cost: 5 units per call (one `users.messages.get` with `format=FULL` to enumerate the MIME part tree).

Feature 004 (read-api-completeness). Requires gmail.readonly scope.

response.name: 200 OK — Message has attachments
200 OK — Message has no attachments (empty list, NOT 404)
404 Not Found — Message does not exist
response.body:
{
    "type": "/problems/resource-not-found",
    "title": "Resource Not Found",
    "status": 404,
    "detail": "Message '0000000000000000' was not found or has been deleted",
    "instance": "/api/v1/gmail/messages/0000000000000000/attachments",
    "retryable": false,
    "category": "CLIENT_ERROR"
}

query.description: Filename to use in Content-Disposition header. Sanitized: CR/LF/NUL/Unicode line-terminators return 400. Defaults to 'attachment' if absent. Obtain from List Attachments response.
MIME type to use as Content-Type. Defaults to 'application/octet-stream' if absent. URL-encode the slash (use %2F). Obtain from List Attachments response.

variable.description: Gmail attachment ID. Must match [A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,1024}. Obtain from List Attachments or Get Thread Detail / Get Message Detail responses.

request.description:
Downloads the raw binary content of a specific attachment. The response body is the decoded binary (base64url-decoded from Gmail's storage format) streamed via `StreamingResponseBody` — no full buffering in JVM heap.

Passing `?filename=` and `?mimeType=` query parameters (obtained from List Attachments) gives the correct `Content-Type` and `Content-Disposition` headers without an extra API call. Without them, the response falls back to `application/octet-stream` and filename `attachment`.

Response headers set on success:
- `Content-Type: <mimeType OR application/octet-stream>`
- `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<filename OR attachment>"` — forces save-as in browsers (defends against XSS via SVG/HTML attachments)
- `Content-Length: <decoded byte count>` — use for progress bars; may differ from `sizeBytes` in List Attachments (which is Gmail's estimate)
- `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` — prevents browser MIME-sniffing

`?filename=` sanitization: values containing CR (`\r`), LF (`\n`), NUL (`\0`), or any Unicode line-terminator return 400 (header injection prevention). Filenames returned by the List Attachments endpoint are always safe to forward.

Path parameter validation: `messageId` must match `[0-9a-fA-F]{1,32}`; `attachmentId` must match `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,1024}`.

Quota cost: 5 units per call (`users.messages.attachments.get`).

Feature 004 (read-api-completeness). Requires gmail.readonly scope.

item.description:
Seven new read-only endpoints introduced by feature 004 (read-api-completeness). All seven endpoints use GET and require gmail.readonly scope (already covered by the gmail.modify scope in the project's OAuth2 configuration). No re-consent is required.

Endpoints in this folder:
1. List Threads — paginated conversation list with optional filter criteria (quota: 10 flat)
2. Get Thread Detail — full conversation with nested messages (quota: 10)
3. Get Message Detail — single message with optional format param (quota: 10 full / 5 metadata)
4. List Labels — all visible labels in one call, no pagination (quota: 1)
5. Get Label Detail — full label metadata with counts and color (quota: 1)
6. List Attachments — attachment metadata on a message without downloading binary (quota: 5)
7. Download Attachment — streamed binary download with Content-Disposition and X-Content-Type-Options (quota: 5)

All endpoints emit X-Gmail-Quota-Used, X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset headers. 429 responses include Retry-After. Error responses use application/problem+json with the existing ProblemDetail shape.

Bearer token for Authorization header. Paste your OAuth2 access token here (starts with ya29.). Obtain via OAuth2 Playground with gmail.send scope: https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground
Set this to the draftId returned by POST /api/v1/gmail/drafts before calling POST /api/v1/gmail/drafts/{draftId}/send
Gmail short message ID of the original message for threading. Obtain via GET /api/v1/gmail/messages/latest. Used by Send Threaded Reply and Send Threaded Reply with Attachment requests.

Gmail thread ID corresponding to originalMsgId. Usually the same value as the message ID for the first message in a thread. Used by Send Threaded Reply and Send Threaded Reply with Attachment requests.
An email address you control (inbox you own) for testing send/draft endpoints.
Standard Base64-encoded content of a test attachment. Default is a minimal 9-byte PDF stub (JVBERi0xLjQK). For a real file: base64 -i yourfile.pdf | tr -d '\n'
